column1: # Column 2: string representation of stack top value(s), space-separated
# When column 3 is present, column 2 is ignored (may be empty).
# upper now has suffix precedence (unified model)
# === Unify - identical scalars (prefix: left right unify) ===
# === Unify - identical scalars (infix: left unify right) ===
# === Unify - type narrowing with parent types ===
# === Unify - parent type with parent type ===
# === Unify - any narrows to parent type ===
# === Unify - none only unifies with itself ===
# Suffix form: unify left right end
# All three forms produce same result
# === Unify - typed lists with identical child types ===
# === Unify - typed lists with concrete lists ===
# === Unify - typed list child type mismatch ===
# === Unify - typed list with type literal ===
# === Unify - typed list with map child type ===
# === Unify - typed maps with identical child types ===
# === Unify - typed maps with concrete maps ===
# === Unify - typed maps with empty concrete map ===
# === Unify - typed maps with concrete (fail - type mismatch) ===
# === Unify - typed map child type mismatch ===
# === Unify - typed map with type literal ===
# === Unify - typed map with map child type ===
# === Unify - deeper typed map child type unification ===
# === Unify - typed map vs incompatible types ===
# Basic def with list body (literal substitution)
def triple [dup dup add add] 4 triple
# def with prefix body (body def name form)
def inc [1 add] 1 inc inc inc
# def does not break existing word coercion
# Wrong type for upper (integer instead of string)
# Too many prefix args for upper
def foo 1 foo undef foo foo
# Nested defs: undef reveals the shadowed definition
def foo 1 def foo 2 foo undef foo foo
# Multiple undefs peel off all layers
def foo 1 def foo 2 def foo 3 foo undef foo foo undef foo foo undef foo foo
(def foo 1) (add foo 2) (def foo 3) (add foo 4) (undef foo) (add foo 5) (undef foo)
# Basic var: take x from stack
# var with multiple variables from stack
# var with mixed inline and stack variables
# var cleans up: word reverts to string after var
7 var [[n] n mul n add n]
# var with single inline value in body
var [[[a 3] [b 4]] a mul a add b mul b]
def foo 99 5 var [[x] x add foo] foo
def square fn [[x:number] [number] [x mul x]] 5 square
def add2 fn [[x:number,y:number] [number] [x add y]] 3 5 add2
# Named parameters don't leak after call
def sq fn [[x:number] [number] [x mul x]] 4 sq x
def sq fn [[x:number] [number] [x mul x]] 4 sq undef sq sq
def foo fn [[x:number] [number] [x mul x]] def foo fn [[x:number,y:number] [number] [x add y]] undef foo fn [[number,number] [number]] 5 foo
def foo fn [[x:number] [number] [x mul x]] def foo fn [[x:number,y:number] [number] [x add y]] undef foo fn [[number] [number]] 3 5 foo
def sq fn [[x:number] [number] [x mul x]] sq 6
def inc fn [[x:number] [number] [x add 1]] 1 inc inc inc
(def foo 42) (def foo fn [[number] [number] [dup mul]]) (3 foo) (undef foo) foo
# Boolean precedence: and binds tighter than or
type Point record [x:number y:number] make Point [1 2]
# make record: named fields in declaration order
type Point record [x:number y:number] make Point [x:1 y:2]
# make record: named fields in different order
type Point record [x:number y:number] make Point [y:3 x:4]
# make record: type conversion applied to fields
type bar record [x:number y:boolean z:string] make bar [1 false "s"]
# make record: named fields reordered with type conversion
type bar record [x:number y:boolean z:string] make bar [y:false x:2 z:"Z"]
# make record: field value conversion (string to number)
type Pair record [a:number b:number] make Pair ["3" "7"]
type P record [x:number y:number] P make [1 2]
type Vec record [x:number y:number] make Vec [3 4]
type OptNum (number or none) OptNum unify 5
type OptNum (number or none) OptNum unify none
type OptNum (number or none) OptNum unify "hi"
# === do - evaluate list and map contents ===
# do list: evaluate as token stream
# do map: mixed list and non-list values
# do map: nested maps with list values
# dblcall doubles integer then invokes callback with result
# callback that just leaves the doubled value
def sq fn [[x:number] [number] [x mul x]] dblcall 3 [sq]
# call splices list contents as code in a paren scope
# empty list call is a no-op (value passes through)
# === def fn style dblcall (passing functions as arguments) ===
# define dblcall accepting a function, doubles n and calls cb with result
def dupmul fn [[x:number] [number] [x dup mul]] def dblcall2 fn [[n:number, cb:function] [number] [n add n cb]] dblcall2 5 dupmul
# def fn dblcall with a different callback function
def dupadd fn [[x:number] [number] [x dup add]] def dblcall2 fn [[n:number, cb:function] [number] [n add n cb]] dblcall2 3 dupadd
# def fn dblcall with identity callback
def id fn [[x:number] [number] [x]] def dblcall2 fn [[n:number, cb:function] [number] [n add n cb]] dblcall2 4 id
